metadata.usage: Pre-NLP fast regex-based section boundary detection
notes: Match at line start with optional whitespace
Patterns ordered from most specific to least specific
OCR variations include common misreads: rn->m, l->1, o->0

format_notes.US: Name + Phone + Email + LinkedIn + City, State
format_notes.UK: Name + Phone + Email + Location (no full address for privacy)
format_notes.India: Name + Phone + Email + LinkedIn + Full Address + DOB sometimes included
format_notes.Europe: May include photo, DOB (GDPR varies by country)
format_notes.Australia: Name + Phone + Email + LinkedIn + Suburb, State

format_notes.US: 3-5 line paragraph, no first-person pronouns in ATS-optimized resumes
format_notes.UK: Personal Statement common for graduates, Profile for experienced
format_notes.India: Career Objective or Professional Summary both widely used
format_notes.Europe: Profile common in UK/EU style CVs
format_notes.Australia: Professional Summary or Career Profile preferred

format_notes.US: Largely replaced by Professional Summary, but still common for entry-level and career changers
format_notes.India: Career Objective is extremely common across all experience levels
format_notes.Canada: Objective still used, especially for new graduates

format_notes.US: Reverse chronological order, bullet points with action verbs + metrics
format_notes.UK: Employment History common, may include reason for leaving
format_notes.India: Work Experience, current job first, may include CTC
format_notes.Europe: Europass format sometimes used, dates on left
format_notes.Australia: Career History, reverse chronological, duties and achievements

format_notes.US: After experience for 3+ years, before experience for new grads
format_notes.UK: Qualifications, Education; A-levels, GCSEs included for junior roles
format_notes.India: Educational Qualifications very common; includes 10th/12th marks, percentage
format_notes.Europe: May follow Europass format with standardized fields

format_notes.US: Bullet points or comma-separated, grouped by category
format_notes.India: Technical Skills prominently featured, often with proficiency levels
format_notes.Europe: May use star ratings or proficiency bars
format_notes.Australia: Core Skills, Key Skills, bullet format

format_notes.US: Certification name, issuing body, date, expiry if applicable
format_notes.UK: Qualifications and Certifications, may merge with Education
format_notes.India: IT certifications (AWS, Microsoft) very prominently listed
format_notes.Australia: Licenses and Certifications, especially for trade/regulatory roles

format_notes.US: Project name, tech stack, description, GitHub/live link
format_notes.India: Final Year Project very prominently listed by graduates
format_notes.UK: Key Projects within experience section or separate
format_notes.Australia: Often merged within experience with highlighted projects

format_notes.India: Achievements used very commonly; includes academic ranks and percentages
format_notes.UK: Distinctions, Prizes common in academic CVs

format_notes.US: APA or MLA citation format, reverse chronological
format_notes.UK: Academic CVs follow Harvard citation style
format_notes.India: Research publications listed for academic/research roles

format_notes.US: Role, organization, dates, impact — treated like work experience
format_notes.India: Social work, NSS/NCC activities common for graduates

format_notes.US: Often merged with Work Experience, separate section for students
format_notes.India: Industrial Training, Summer Internship are standard sections
format_notes.UK: Work Placement, Year in Industry (for sandwich degrees)
format_notes.Australia: Practical placement sections common for professional degrees

format_notes.US: Language name + proficiency level (Native, Fluent, Professional, Conversational)
format_notes.Europe: CEFR levels (A1-C2) common, especially in Europass
format_notes.India: Languages Known section with read/write/speak columns

format_notes.US: Often omitted in professional resumes; bullet list if included
format_notes.UK: Interests section more common in UK CVs
format_notes.India: Hobbies and Interests almost always included
format_notes.Europe: Personal Interests common in Europass and German-style CVs

format_notes.US: Often just 'References available upon request' or omitted entirely
format_notes.UK: Referees section with 2 contacts is common
format_notes.India: References with full contact details often included
format_notes.Australia: Referees section with 2-3 professional contacts standard

format_notes.US: Common in executive resumes, MBA applications
format_notes.India: Leadership roles in college clubs/societies often listed
format_notes.UK: Leadership roles within work experience section

format_notes.US: Often listed under Certifications or separate Training section
format_notes.UK: CPD (Continuing Professional Development) is a standard term
format_notes.Australia: Professional Development important in regulated industries

format_notes.US: Clearance level (Secret, TS, TS/SCI), active/inactive status
format_notes.UK: SC, DV, CTC clearances common for government roles
format_notes.Australia: NV1, NV2, PV clearances for defence roles
format_notes.Canada: Reliability Status, Secret, Top Secret levels

format_notes.UK: Professional body membership (CIPD, CIMA, etc.) very important
format_notes.India: Professional body memberships listed for engineering/accounting roles
format_notes.Australia: Professional associations prominent in trade/licensed occupations

declaration.notes: Very common in Indian resumes, not used elsewhere

personal_details_india.notes: India-specific personal details often included (considered inappropriate in US/UK/Australia)

python_implementation.step_2: For each line, check max_line_length_for_header (80 chars) and max_words (8 words)
python_implementation.step_3: Apply regex patterns with re.IGNORECASE and re.MULTILINE flags
python_implementation.step_4: Check quick_lookup_index for fast O(1) alias matching
python_implementation.step_5: Apply confidence_scoring boosts based on formatting (all caps, bold, font size)
python_implementation.step_7: Validate by checking content exists below detected header
python_implementation.step_8: Pass detected sections to NLP layer for deeper analysis
